bids.reasoning: Since the negotiation is just starting (time_fraction = 0.0), I will open with the my highest-utility offer to anchor the negotiation. This means choosing the highest-utility value ('valueE') for every issue, setting my initial utility to 1.0. This leaves room to concede on the less critical issues (like issueA and issueB) later if needed.

We are extremely early in the negotiation (t=0.02) and the opponent's "win-win" baseline offer actually provides me with my absolute lowest possible utility. To constructively move the conversation forward without giving up too much ground, I will maintain my top-value requests on my most critical issues (issueC, issueD, issueE) while making a small concession on my lowest-priority issue (issueA).

The current time is very early in the negotiation (t=0.04), and the opponent has repeated their opening maximal offer while using cheap talk about fairness. Since my weight for issueA and issueB is the lowest, I will begin my concessions there to invite trades. My new bid maintains my highest preferred values on the highly weighted issues (C, D, and E) while dropping issueA slightly further to show willingness to move.

I will continue to make small, gradual concessions on my lowest-priority issues (issueB, weight 0.1) while maintaining a firm stance on my heavily weighted issues (issueC, issueD, issueE).

I will carefully step down on my lowest-weighted issues (issueB and issueA) to test the opponent's true willingness to inch towards a mutually beneficial agreement while holding the line on my top priorities.

The negotiation is still early (t=0.14) and the opponent's offers are slowly improving in my highest weight issues (issueD and issueE).
